LAW AND MOTION 302, "IN RESPONSE TO THE COVID-19 OUTBREAK, THE HEARING DATES FOR ALL LAW AND MOTION AND DISCOVERY MATTERS CURRENTLY CALENDARED IN DEPARTMENTS 301/302 ARE HEREBY VACATED. WHEN THE COURT RESUMES NORMAL OPERATIONS, THE PRESIDING JUDGE WILL ANNOUNCE PROCEDURES FOR SETTING HEARING DATES FOR THOSE CASES WHICH HAVE BEEN FULLY BRIEFED. OPPOSITIONS WILL BE DUE BASED ON THE RESCHEDULED HEARING DATE, BUT NOTHING PRECLUDES A PARTY FROM FILING AN OPPOSITION EARLIER." (302)

Notice Of Motion Of Defendants Cross-Complainants Miranda Boanerges And Horlino To Contest Platitiff Alicia Gonzaless Application For Good Faith Determination And Request For Continuance
Off Calendar Feb-18-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
David Lavine, Esq. of HARRINGTON FOXX DUBROW & CANTER LLP, T:415.288.6600, for cross defendant/moving party Saquilayan;
Edgar Hawkyard, Esq. of the LAW OFFICES OF CATHERINE WALSH, T:925.825.9500, for cross complainant Boanerges & Horlino, on Court Call.

RULING: The tentative ruling was contested and argued. After argumets, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANTs HORLINO TABIOS, and BOANERGES MIRANDA's Motion To Contest Platitiff Alicia Gonzales' Application For Good Faith Determination And Request For Continuance as follows:

Defendants Boanerges Miranda and Horlino Tabios' motion to contest good faith settlement is granted. While the party asserting the lack of good faith has the burden on that issue (Code Civ. Proc. 877.6(d)), the settlors must provide the court with a sufficient evidentiary basis upon which to make a good faith determination. (See Code Civ. Proc. 877.6(b) ["The issue of the good faith of a settlement may be determined on the basis of affidavits served with the notice of hearing, and any counteraffidavits filed in response . . ."].) "If 'there is no substantial evidence to support a critical assumption as to the nature and extent of a settling defendant's liability, then a determination of good faith based upon such assumption is an abuse of discretion.'" (Mattco Forge, Inc. v. Arthur Young & Co. (1995) 38 Cal.App.4th 1337, 1348, 1350 at n.6 [settling parties did not proffer any evidence regarding their proportionate liability for plaintiff's alleged injury, but merely presented a series of "questionable assumptions" in their memorandum to show the settlement amount was reasonable].)

Here, settlors fail to present any evidence that would enable the court to determine "a rough approximation of plaintiffs' total recovery and the settlor's proportionate liability." (Tech-Bilt, Inc. v. Woodward-Clyde & Associates (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488, 499.) The traffic collision report, which is based on hearsay, is not sufficient by itself for the settlors to maintain their burden.

Mr. Hawkyard to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the Court's ruling and submit it in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Cross-Defendant Eloisa Legaspi Saquilayan From Cross-Complaint
Off Calendar Feb-18-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
David Lavine, Esq. of HARRINGTON FOXX DUBROW & CANTER LLP, T:415.288.6600, for cross defendant/moving party Saquilayan;
Edgar Hawkyard, Esq. of the LAW OFFICES OF CATHERINE WALSH, T:925.825.9500, for cross complainant Boanerges & Horlino, on Court Call.

RULING: The tentative ruling was contested. After hearing, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANT ELOISA SAQUILAYAN's Motion To Dismiss Cross-Defendant Eloisa Legaspi Saquilayan From Cross-Complaint as follows:

Eloisa Legaspi Saquilayan's motion to dismiss cross-complaint is denied as moot based on the ruling in the companion motion.

Mr. Hawkyard to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the Court's ruling and submit it in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

Amended Motion To Vacate Order Granting Application For Order Of Good Faith Settlement Determination
Off Calendar Jan-15-2020 Text Ruling

Motion To Vacate Order Granting Application For Order Of Good Faith Settlement Determination
Off Calendar Jan-15-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Robert A. Bazzo, Esq. of FREEMAN MATHIS & GARY LLP, T:213.615.7000, for defendant, on Court Call;
No other appearance.

RULING: There being no objection, the tentative ruling on DEFENDANTS HORLINO TABIOS, BOANERGES MIRANDA's Motion To Vacate Order Granting Application For Order Of Good Faith Settlement Determination is adopted as follows:

The Court exercises its discretion to reconsider its order granting the application for good faith settlement determination between Plaintiff Alicia Gonzales and Defendant Eloisa Legaspi Saquilayan. (See Le Francois v. Goel (2005) 35 Cal.4th 1094.) Defendants Miranda and Tabios must file a motion to contest the good faith determination no later than January 29, 2020. The motion for reconsideration of the September 18, 2019 order will be heard on February 18, 2020 in Department 302 at 9:30 a.m.

Counsel for the movants is to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and submit it in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.
